Which property of a feature class defines whether it stores points, lines, or polygons?
Where can you see the full path and type of a layer's data source?
What is a shapefile's main limitation compared to a feature class?

options.text: Field name length and number of fields are limited

questions.text: Which operation creates a new feature class from selected features?
What does the Define Projection tool do?

options.text: Assigns a coordinate system to data that has none or wrong one
Reprojects data to a new coordinate system
Sets the map's display coordinate system
Exports the coordinate system to a file

questions.text: Which data format can store topology rules and relationship classes?
How do you add a field to a feature class table in ArcGIS Pro?

options.text: In the attribute table, click Add Field
Use the Add Field geoprocessing tool or table Design view
Use the Calculate Field tool only

questions.text: What type of data does a raster dataset typically represent?

options.text: Cell-based values such as elevation or imagery

questions.text: Where do you set the default geodatabase for a project?
Which statement about saving edits in ArcGIS Pro is true?

options.text: You must save or discard edits before ending the edit session
Saving the project saves all layer edits
Edits cannot be undone after save

questions.text: What is the purpose of the Catalog pane in ArcGIS Pro?

options.text: To organize and access data sources, add data, and manage items

questions.text: Which file is the main entry point for an ArcGIS Pro project?
What does the Merge tool do?

options.text: Combines multiple feature classes into one
Merges selected features within a single layer

questions.text: Which storage can hold multiple feature classes and tables in one container?
How can you restrict attribute values when editing a feature class?

options.text: Using subtypes and domains in the geodatabase
By using a layer file only

questions.text: What is an attribute domain?

options.text: The coordinate system of the feature class
A set of valid values or range for a field

questions.text: Which action copies a feature class from one geodatabase to another?

options.text: Copy tool in Catalog pane or Copy geoprocessing tool

questions.text: What does the Append tool do?

options.text: Adds features from one dataset into an existing target dataset
Creates a new empty feature class

questions.text: Where do you view the coordinate system of the current map?
Which data source can be added directly to a map in ArcGIS Pro?

options.text: Feature classes, shapefiles, rasters, and tables from supported sources

questions.text: What is the role of the Shape field in a feature class?

options.text: Stores the geometry type name only
Stores the geographic coordinates and shape of each feature

questions.text: Which geodatabase component enforces rules about how features share boundaries?
How do you create a new point feature class in a file geodatabase?

options.text: Use Create Feature Class tool and set geometry type to Point
Use Add Data and choose Point

questions.text: Which pane lists the layers in the current map?
Where do you change the symbology of a layer?
What does the Primary symbology type 'Single Symbol' do?

options.text: Draws all features with the same symbol

questions.text: Which symbology method is used to show quantities with color gradation?

options.text: Match to symbols in a style

questions.text: How do you add labels to features on the map?

options.text: Layer Properties, Labeling tab; define label expression and enable labeling
Use the Label tool in Edit

questions.text: What is the purpose of the Map Frame on a layout?

options.text: It displays the map view in the layout

questions.text: Which view do you use to create a printable map with title, legend, and scale bar?
Where is the ribbon that contains Map and Layer contextual tabs?

options.text: At the top of the ArcGIS Pro application

questions.text: What does 'Unique Values' symbology do?

options.text: Assigns a different symbol to each distinct attribute value
Uses one symbol for all features
Shows only unique features by location

questions.text: How do you change the order of layers drawing on the map?

options.text: Drag layers up or down in the Contents pane

questions.text: Which control adjusts the visibility of a layer at certain map scales?
What is the Contents pane used for?

options.text: Managing layers, symbol visibility, and drawing order

questions.text: Where do you set the font and size for feature labels?
What does the Basemap provide on a map?

options.text: Reference context such as streets or imagery

questions.text: How do you open the Symbology pane for a layer?

options.text: Select the layer and use the Symbology button on the Appearance tab or Layer Properties

questions.text: Which option lets you show a subset of features without deleting them?
What is a bookmark used for?

options.text: Saving a specific map extent and scale for quick return

questions.text: Where do you set the transparency of a layer?
What does 'Graduated Symbols' symbology show?

options.text: Symbol size varies by numeric attribute value

questions.text: Which pane shows the attribute table for a layer?

options.text: Attribute table window (opened from layer context menu or View tab)

questions.text: How do you add a scale bar to a layout?
What is the purpose of the Explore tool?

options.text: Pan and click to identify or pop open a popup

questions.text: Where do you add a north arrow to a layout?
Which tab appears when a map is active and you select a layer?

options.text: Feature Layer or contextual layer tabs (e.g. Appearance, Labeling)

questions.text: What does the label expression control?

options.text: Which field or formula is used for the label text

questions.text: How do you display a legend on a layout?
What is the difference between Map view and Layout view?

options.text: Map view shows the data; Layout view arranges map and elements for export
Map view is for editing only

questions.text: Where do you set the reference scale for a data frame or map frame?

options.text: Map frame properties or map properties (reference scale)

questions.text: Which symbology type is best for categories like land use or type?
What does the Full Extent button do?

options.text: Zooms the map to show all layers' extent

questions.text: How do you change the color of a symbol in the Symbology pane?

options.text: Click the symbol color patch and choose a new color

questions.text: What is the role of the Map tab on the ribbon?

options.text: Navigate map, add data, create bookmarks, and access map-related tools

questions.text: Which element on a layout shows the ratio of map distance to real-world distance?
Where do you control whether labels are placed for the whole layer or only selected features?

options.text: Labeling tab or Layer Properties – Labeling options

questions.text: How do you add a title or text to a layout?

options.text: Insert tab, Text or Rectangle Text

questions.text: Which view allows you to zoom and pan the map interactively?

options.text: Layout view only (no pan/zoom on map)

questions.text: Which format can you export a layout to for sharing a static map?

options.text: PDF, PNG, JPEG, and others via Share tab or Export Layout

questions.text: What does sharing a map as a web map allow recipients to do?

options.text: View and interact with the map in a browser or ArcGIS apps

questions.text: Where do you start the process to share a web layer from ArcGIS Pro?

options.text: Share tab – Share As Web Layer or similar

questions.text: What is a package (.mpkx or .ppkx) used for?

options.text: Packaging a project or map with data for sharing or backup

questions.text: When would you use 'Share As Web Map' versus exporting to PDF?

options.text: Web map for interactive viewing online; PDF for static print or file share

questions.text: Which item must you have to share content to ArcGIS Online or portal?

options.text: An organizational account and connection to the portal

questions.text: What does Export Map produce?

options.text: An image or file of the current map view (or layout)

questions.text: Where do you set the page size and orientation for a layout?
What is the purpose of creating a map package?

options.text: To share a map and its data in one portable file

questions.text: Which sharing method preserves interactivity (zoom, popups) for online use?

options.text: Publishing as a web map or web layer

questions.text: How do you add a new layout to a project?

options.text: Insert tab, New Layout, or right-click Layouts in Catalog

questions.text: What is the difference between a layout and a map?

options.text: A map holds the data layers; a layout arranges that map and elements for printing or export

questions.text: Which tool finds features within a specified distance of other features?

options.text: Buffer or Select Layer By Location (within a distance)

questions.text: What does the Buffer tool create?

options.text: Polygon zones at a specified distance around features

questions.text: How do you select features by clicking on the map?

options.text: Use the Select tool and click features, or use the selection tools on the Map tab

questions.text: Which method selects features that have an attribute matching a value or expression?
What does the Intersect tool do?

options.text: Computes geometric intersection of overlapping layers

questions.text: When would you use Select Layer By Location?

options.text: To select features based on spatial relationship to another layer
To select by attribute value only

questions.text: Which overlay tool keeps only the area where two polygon layers overlap?
What does the Union tool do?

options.text: Combines geometries of two layers into one, preserving all areas

questions.text: How do you clear the current selection?

options.text: Clear selection button (Map or Attribute table) or Selection menu

questions.text: Which tool extracts features that fall inside the boundaries of another layer?
What is the correct workflow to find parcels within 500 m of a river?

options.text: Use Select Layer By Location (parcels within distance of river) or Buffer river then spatial selection

questions.text: Where do you run geoprocessing tools in ArcGIS Pro?
Which selection method uses a SQL-like expression?
What does the Clip tool require as input?

options.text: Input features and clip features (boundary layer)

questions.text: When would you use Buffer before an overlay analysis?

options.text: To create a zone of influence, then intersect or select with other layers

questions.text: How do you select all features that intersect another layer?

options.text: Select Layer By Location, spatial relationship 'intersect' or 'have their centroid in'

questions.text: Which tool would you use to combine adjacent polygons with the same attribute?
What is the purpose of the Select tool on the Map tab?

options.text: To select features interactively by clicking or drawing a shape

questions.text: Which workflow finds points that fall inside polygons?

options.text: Select Layer By Location – target points, source polygons, 'within' or 'contained in'

questions.text: What does 'spatial relationship' in Select Layer By Location define?

options.text: How target features relate to source features (intersect, within, contain, etc.)
